disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden onset of severe one-sided lower pelvic pain that began abruptly during physical exertion (squats at the gym)
trigger_question_examples: When and how did the pain start?
What were you doing when it came on?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It hit me all of a sudden while I was doing squats — one second I was fine, the next this stabbing pain in my left side dropped me.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pain was initially intermittent and colicky and has become severe and constant
trigger_question_examples: Has the pain changed since it started?
Is it constant or does it come and go?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: At first it kind of came in waves, like a cramp, but now it's just there all the time and it's really bad.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Severe nausea with recurrent vomiting since pain onset
trigger_question_examples: Have you been sick or nauseous?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I feel so sick and I keep throwing up — I can't stop.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pain sharply exacerbated by movement and changing position
trigger_question_examples: Does anything make the pain worse?
What happens when you move or try to sit up?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Every time I try to move or sit up it stabs even worse, so I'm just staying dead still.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever and no chills; the illness was not preceded by feeling generally unwell
trigger_question_examples: Have you had any fever or chills?
Were you feeling unwell before the pain started?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I haven't felt hot or had the shivers, and I was totally fine right up until the pain hit.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Menstrual and pregnancy context — periods are regular, no abnormal vaginal bleeding, patient states pregnancy is not expected
trigger_question_examples: When was your last menstrual period?
Is there any chance you could be pregnant?
Have you had any abnormal vaginal bleeding?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My periods are regular and my last one was a couple of weeks ago. I don't think I could be pregnant, and I haven't had any unusual bleeding.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: Why does that matter? It's my side that hurts, not anything to do with my period.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No blood in the urine and no pain spreading to the flank or groin
trigger_question_examples: Have you noticed any blood when you pass urine?
Does the pain spread around to your back or down to your groin?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No blood that I've seen, and the pain isn't going round to my back — it's all low down at the front.
